title: calamus aruensis
intro: Calamus aruensis is a species of plant native to the island of New Guinea, belonging to the family Zingiberaceae, and is also known as lesser calamus or sweet calamus. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Calamus aruensis include relief from digestive issues, such as bloating and nausea, as well as its potential to reduce anxiety and improve sleep quality. Calamus aruensis can be prepared as an herbal tea, essential oil, or tincture, which are often used to treat various health conditions, and its root is also used in traditional cooking and as a spice in some local cuisine.
